target.title: Add per-agent sandbox session visibility override feature
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: sandbox session tools resolved only the default sandbox visibility clamp, so per-agent `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` could be configured but not reliably honored by the session tools.
- Why it matters: operators need to grant one named sandboxed agent broader or narrower session visibility without changing every sandboxed agent.
- What changed: requester-aware visibility resolution now checks the matching per-agent sandbox override before falling back to `agents.defaults.sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility`, and `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, `sessions_send`, and `session_status` pass the requester identity through that path.
- What did not change: existing configs keep the same default behavior unless a per-agent override is explicitly set.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Plugin SDK / public config surface
- [x] Docs / changelog

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

New optional per-agent config field:

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "list": [
      { "id": "tony", "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "all" } }
    ],
    "defaults": {
      "sandbox": { "sessionToolsVisibility": "spawned" }
    }
  }
}
```

No behavior change for agents without the field.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes, opt-in per-agent session visibility can broaden or narrow what a named sandboxed agent sees.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No.
- New/changed network calls? No.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No.
- Data access scope changed? Yes, explicit per named agent only.
- Risk + mitigation: global default remains unchanged; broader visibility requires an explicit named-agent override and is covered by access-layer and tool-level tests.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? New optional config field only.
- Migration needed? No.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- Remove `sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` from the agent list entry to fall back to the default sandbox session visibility.
- If visibility is broader than expected, inspect `agents.list[].sandbox.sessionToolsVisibility` before the defaults entry.

## Files in this PR (matches GitHub head)

- `CHANGELOG.md` — records the user-facing runtime/config behavior change.
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md` — documents where operators configure per-agent sandbox visibility.
- `docs/gateway/config-tools.md` — documents session tool visibility and per-agent override precedence.
- `docs/tools/multi-agent-sandbox-tools.md` — documents sandbox session tool behavior for multi-agent setups.
- `src/plugin-sdk/session-visibility.ts` — owns sandbox session visibility resolution and now accepts requester agent identity.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts` — wires requester identity into the affected session tool implementations.
- `src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts` — makes `session_status` use the same requester-aware visibility rules.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.ts` — carries requester context through spawned/self/tree/all enforcement.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-helpers.ts` — forwards requester identity through shared session helper wiring.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-history-tool.ts` — applies requester-aware visibility to session history.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.ts` — applies requester-aware visibility to session listing.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.ts` — applies requester-aware visibility to session send checks.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts` — proves `session_status` follows requester-aware visibility.
- `src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts` — proves `createOpenClawTools` wires requester visibility correctly.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts` — proves access-layer resolution honors per-agent sandbox overrides and fallback.
- `src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts` — proves end-to-end session tool behavior through the existing sessions test surface.

Note: `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` was allowed during repair, but after rebasing onto current upstream the generated API checksum already matches upstream and is no longer in the PR diff.

## Evidence

- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` on the 16 touched files.
- [x] `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`.
- [x] `pnpm test -- src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions-access.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions-visibility.test.ts src/agents/tools/sessions.test.ts`.
- [x] `pnpm check:test-types`.
- [x] `pnpm lint --threads=8`.
- [x] `git diff --check`.
- [x] GitHub PR file list checked against the approved scope: 16 files, no extras.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: operator accidentally grants broad visibility to the wrong agent.
- Mitigation: the override is explicit per named agent, default behavior is unchanged, and access/tool tests cover override and fallback behavior.

target.title: fix(feishu): guard against undefined receiveIdType in resolveFeishuSendTarget
target.body:
## What

resolveReceiveIdType() previously fell back to "user_id" for any unrecognized ID format, silently sending the wrong ID type to the Feishu API and causing 400 "receive_id_type is required" errors on newer Lark SDK versions (which silently drop undefined fields).

Now returns null for unrecognized formats, forcing all callers to handle the error explicitly with a descriptive throw rather than propagating invalid state.

## Root Cause

Bot review correctly identified that resolveReceiveIdType never returned falsy — it unconditionally returned "user_id" as a fallback. Unknown ID formats were silently treated as user_id, which the Feishu API would then reject.

## How

- targets.ts: resolveReceiveIdType returns null | "chat_id" | "open_id" | "user_id" — returns null instead of "user_id" for unrecognized formats
- send-target.ts: guard + throw when receiveIdType is null
- reply-dispatcher.ts: guard + early return when receiveIdType is null

Fixes #53376

target.title: fix(media): sanitize fetch errors before posting to channels
target.body:
## Summary

When a media fetch operation fails, the raw error message ├ö├ç├Â which may contain serialized JSON with private group IDs, phone numbers, user names, and internal system paths ├ö├ç├Â was posted to the configured channel as if it were a normal reply. This exposed PII to public-facing channels like Discord and Telegram.

**Example of what was leaking:**
```
Media failed: {"group_id":"@g.us","sender":"+905...","name":"John Doe","path":"/home/user/.openclaw/..."}
```

## Details

Added `SAFE_MEDIA_FETCH_ERROR_MESSAGE` constant and `isSafeMediaFetchError()` helper to `src/media/fetch.ts`. When a `MediaFetchError` is detected at the outbound delivery layer (`message-action-params.ts`, `message-action-runner.ts`, `deliver.ts`), the raw error is replaced with:

```
├ö├£├í┬┤┬®├à Media fetch failed. The attachment could not be retrieved.
```

The original error is still logged internally via `logVerbose` ├ö├ç├Â it is only redacted from the outbound channel payload.

Three sanitization boundary points:
1. `message-action-params.ts` ├ö├ç├Â params resolution for media actions
2. `message-action-runner.ts` ├ö├ç├Â action execution runner
3. `deliver.ts` ├ö├ç├Â final delivery layer

## Related Issues

Fixes #20279

## How to Validate

Trigger a media fetch that fails (invalid URL or timeout). Confirm:
- Channel receives the generic safe message
- `logVerbose` / internal logs still contain the full error

Run unit tests:
```bash
pnpm test -- --testPathPattern="fetch|message-action-runner.media"
```

Note: `message-action-runner.media.test.ts` requires `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` which was added to `main` today (commit `6e20c4baa0`). The test will pass once dependencies are installed in CI. The `fetch.test.ts` suite (12 tests) passes locally and covers the core PII sanitization behavior.

## Pre-Merge Checklist

- [x] Updated relevant documentation and README (if needed)
- [x] Added/updated tests (if needed)
- [ ] Noted breaking changes (if any)
- [x] Validated on required platforms/methods:
  - [x] Windows
    - [x] npm run

---
## AI-Assisted Disclosure

- [x] This PR was built with Claude Code (Anthropic)
- [x] Fully tested locally ÔÇö tests pass, oxfmt formatting verified
- [x] Author reviewed and understands all changes
- [x] Codex review not available locally (not installed)

target.title: fix(models): log warning and continue when one provider fails validation
target.body:
## Summary

When `models.providers` contains a validation error in any single provider (e.g., a custom `models` array with a missing `apiKey`), the entire `models.json` config was silently rejected. This caused all custom `baseUrl` overrides and model definitions to be lost without any warning ├ö├ç├Â often unnoticed until requests started failing against the wrong endpoints.

## Details

Added per-provider validation isolation in `src/config/validation.ts`. When a provider-level validation error is detected:
- A clear warning is logged identifying the provider key and the specific validation error
- The invalid provider is skipped
- All other valid providers continue to load normally

**Before:**
```
[silent] All custom models and baseUrl overrides lost
```

**After:**
```
├ö├£├í┬┤┬®├à Provider "minimax" failed validation: "apiKey" is required when defining custom models. Skipping this provider.
```

## Related Issues

Fixes #21584

## How to Validate

1. Configure two providers: one valid (with custom `baseUrl`), one invalid (missing `apiKey`)
2. Start the gateway
3. Confirm the valid provider uses its configured `baseUrl`
4. Check logs ├ö├ç├Â warning shows which provider was skipped and why

Run unit tests: `pnpm test -- --testPathPattern=validation.provider-sanitize`

## Pre-Merge Checklist

- [x] Updated relevant documentation and README (if needed)
- [x] Added/updated tests (if needed)
- [ ] Noted breaking changes (if any)
- [x] Validated on required platforms/methods:
  - [x] Windows
    - [x] npm run

---
## AI-Assisted Disclosure

- [x] This PR was built with Claude Code (Anthropic)
- [x] Fully tested locally ÔÇö tests pass, oxfmt formatting verified
- [x] Author reviewed and understands all changes

target.title: fix(paths): expand env placeholders in resolveUserPath
target.body:
## Summary
Fix path resolution so environment placeholders are expanded before filesystem resolution.

This addresses issue #53628 where `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME}` in configured path strings was treated as a literal directory name.

## Changes
- Add environment placeholder expansion in `src/infra/home-dir.ts`.
  - Support `${VAR_NAME}` and `$VAR_NAME` forms.
  - Keep unknown placeholders unchanged.
  - Preserve existing `~` home expansion behavior.
- Add tests in `src/infra/home-dir.test.ts`:
  - braced env placeholder expansion
  - bare env placeholder expansion
  - unknown placeholder passthrough

## Why this fixes the bug
`resolveUserPath` delegates to `resolveHomeRelativePath`. After this change, inputs like:
- `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME}/workspace/skills`
- `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/workspace/skills`
are resolved using `process.env.XDG_CONFIG_HOME` (or provided env), so skill installation no longer writes into a literal `${XDG_CONFIG_HOME}` folder.

## Testing
```bash
npx vitest run src/infra/home-dir.test.ts src/utils.test.ts
```
Result: pass (`3 files, 51 tests`).

## Risk / boundaries
- Scope is limited to path normalization helpers.
- Unknown env placeholders are intentionally left unchanged.
- Existing behavior for plain paths and tilde expansion is preserved.

target.title: feat(gateway): add watchdog + startup error diagnostics (closes #53684)
target.body:
## Summary

Implements **#53684 - Gateway failure recovery and notification mechanism**.

The PR adds three complementary layers to ensure the OpenClaw Gateway can recover from crashes and notify operators:

### 1. Startup error diagnostics ()

When an uncaught exception or fatal unhandled rejection occurs before or during gateway startup, the error is written to  *before* the process exits. This makes post-mortem diagnosis reliable — the error file survives even when the terminal buffer is gone.

### 2. External watchdog ()

A lightweight bash watchdog designed to be run via  (macOS) or  (Linux). It polls the gateway's  endpoint and:
- Attempts a restart (up to 3 tries) if the gateway is down
- Sends a **macOS Notification** () or **Linux Notification** () when things go wrong
- Logs all events to 

Run it with:
```bash
openclaw gateway watchdog --interval 3
```

### 3. CLI integration

`openclaw gateway watchdog --interval 5` spawns the watchdog script as a foreground child process, forwarding stdout/stderr and handling SIGINT/SIGTERM cleanly.

---

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/infra/startup-error.ts` | New — writes startup errors to `logs/startup-error.txt` |
| `src/infra/unhandled-rejections.ts` | Wire `writeStartupError` into fatal unhandled rejections |
| `src/cli/run-main.ts` | Write startup errors for pre-gateway uncaught exceptions |
| `src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts` | Add `gateway watchdog` subcommand |
| `src/cli/gateway-cli/register.ts` | Register watchdog command |
| `scripts/openclaw-watchdog.sh` | New — bash watchdog script |
| `src/infra/startup-error.ts` | Fix lint: unused `format` import removed |

## Testing

```bash
# Local build
pnpm install && pnpm run build

# Run the watchdog (foreground test)
openclaw gateway watchdog --interval 1 --max-attempts 1 --no-notify

# Check startup error file (after forcing a crash):
# ~/.openclaw/logs/startup-error.txt
```

---

cc @openclaw/maintainers

target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Refs #77969
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

TTS can turn emoji and decorative symbols into noisy spoken names. Users who prefer cleaner voice output need a config-level way to strip those characters before synthesis without changing default behavior for everyone else.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `tts-skip-v3`, commit `06545b841d08`, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (`extensions/microsoft` -> `node-edge-tts` -> `speech.platform.bing.com`). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod`
- After-fix evidence:
  ```text
  $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
  # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
  # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
  TS
  {
    "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
    "commit": "06545b841d08",
    "provider": "microsoft",
    "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
    "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
    "observed": [
      {
        "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
        "bytes": 39456,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      },
      {
        "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
        "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
        "success": true,
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
        "target": "audio-file",
        "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true.mp3",
        "bytes": 36288,
        "headerHex": "fff364c4"
      }
    ],
    "emptyAfterStrip": {
      "success": false,
      "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
      "provider": null
    },
    "artifactsDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof"
  }

  $ ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration,size ...
  ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

  $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       32 passed (32)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests       14 passed (14)

  $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
  exit_code=0
  ```
- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing `✓`, `©`, and `🫠`; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

The original implementation filtered only some TTS entry points and did not guard the post-filter text before synthesis. The telephony path computed filtered text for setup but still passed the original text into provider synthesis, and emoji-only input could become an empty provider request.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts`
- Locked-in scenarios:
  - file synthesis strips complete emoji/symbol sequences when enabled.
  - stream synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - telephony synthesis receives stripped text when enabled.
  - telephony synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
  - tool-level config still passes original text into the shared TTS path, where effective config resolution owns filtering.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users can enable `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols` to strip emoji/symbol characters before speech synthesis. Unset or `false` preserves existing behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
text -> effective TTS config -> optional emoji/symbol strip -> empty guard -> provider synthesis
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- No security-impacting changes identified.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: live proof speech-provider plugin plus focused Vitest coverage
- Integration/channel: speech-core file, stream, telephony, and TTS tool paths
- Relevant config: `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols=true|false`

### Steps

1. Run the live proof script shown above.
2. Run focused speech-core tests.
3. Run focused TTS tool tests.
4. Run test and production type gates.

### Expected

Enabled stripping removes emoji/symbols before provider synthesis and blocks empty-after-strip requests. Disabled mode preserves original text.

### Actual

The proof and tests match the expected behavior.

## Evidence

Included evidence:

- Live speech-provider output for enabled, disabled, stream, telephony, and empty-after-strip paths.
- Focused speech-core test output.
- Focused TTS tool test output.
- Test and production typecheck output.

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: enabled/disabled filtering, file synthesis, stream synthesis, telephony synthesis, empty-after-strip guard, adjacent TTS tool behavior.
- Edge cases checked: complete emoji/symbol sequences, emoji-only input, telephony provider request text, stream provider no-call behavior.
- Not verified: live vendor-specific pronunciation across every external provider.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Upgrade steps: optional new config `messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols`; no change required when unset.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Unicode stripping removes characters a user wanted spoken.
- Mitigation: behavior is opt-in and disabled by default.
- Risk: filtering differs across TTS paths.
- Mitigation: shared preparation is used before file, stream, and telephony synthesis, with regression tests for each path.

target.title: fix: preserve approved heredoc commands on gateway exec
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve approved heredoc commands as-authored in the gateway exec approval flow
- avoid routing approved heredoc commands through the rebuilt allowlist wrapper command
- fixes approval-gated multiline commands like `osascript <<'APPLESCRIPT' ... APPLESCRIPT` failing with `No such file or directory`

## Root cause
The gateway exec approval path rebuilt allowlist-satisfied commands into `enforcedCommand`. That rebuild does not preserve heredoc bodies correctly, so once a heredoc command was approved it could execute in a mangled form.

## Fix
When the command required heredoc-specific approval, execute the original command text (`params.command`) instead of the rebuilt wrapper command. Keep the rebuilt command path for non-heredoc cases.

## Validation
- reproduced locally with an approval-gated AppleScript heredoc
- smoke-tested plain shell heredocs and `osascript` heredocs after the fix

target.title: fix(install): add pipe guard to prevent stdin stealing in curl | bash
target.body:
## Problem

When invoked via `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`, bash reads the script incrementally from stdin. Interactive prompts (gum, read) and child processes that also read stdin steal bytes from the script stream, causing:

1. Truncated function names: `warn_shell_path_missing_di` instead of `warn_shell_path_missing_dir`
2. "command not found" errors for the truncated names
3. Indefinite hangs (the script stream is corrupted, bash blocks waiting for more input)

Reported on WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 with 3/3 reproduction rate.

## Fix

Add a pipe guard at the top of `install.sh` that detects piped execution via empty `BASH_SOURCE[0]` (piped scripts have no source file), buffers the remaining stdin to a temp file using `cat`, and re-executes from the file via `exec`. This frees stdin for interactive prompts and child processes.

The guard:
- **Does not fire** for direct execution (`bash install.sh`) or process substitution (`bash <(curl ...)`) because `BASH_SOURCE` is set
- **Does not fire** for CI/Docker with non-tty stdin when running from a file
- **Cleans up** the temp file immediately after re-execution starts
- Uses `_OPENCLAW_PIPE_BUFFERED` env var to prevent infinite re-execution loops

Fixes #73814

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: the installer now buffers itself to a temp file when piped via curl, preventing child processes from consuming stdin bytes that corrupt the script stream.
Real environment tested: Linux x86_64, OpenClaw 2026.5.12 installed, Node v26.0.0, bash 5.2. Tested both piped execution (`cat install.sh | bash -s -- --install-method npm`) and direct execution (`bash install.sh --install-method npm < /dev/null`).
Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) verified pipe guard pattern with a minimal test script that includes a deliberate stdin steal (`head -c 50 < /dev/stdin`); (2) ran full installer in piped mode; (3) ran full installer in direct mode with non-tty stdin; (4) confirmed both complete successfully without truncation.
Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

Pipe guard pattern verification (minimal test script with deliberate stdin steal):
```
$ cat /tmp/pipe-test-steal.sh | bash
Before stdin steal
After stdin steal
Function executed correctly
Script completed
```
Without the guard, the `head -c 50` would consume 50 bytes of the script stream, truncating the function definition. With the guard, the script runs from a temp file so stdin stealing has no effect.

Full installer in piped mode (script runs from temp file):
```
$ cat scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --install-method npm 2>&1 | tail -10
✓ OpenClaw installed

[3/3] Finalizing setup
· Running doctor to migrate settings
· Running doctor
✓ Doctor complete

🦞 OpenClaw installed successfully (2026.5.12)!
```
Note: error source shows `/tmp/tmp.uuj4JcpyzQ: line 2988` confirming the guard buffered to temp file.

Full installer in direct mode (BASH_SOURCE is set, guard does not fire):
```
$ bash scripts/install.sh --install-method npm < /dev/null 2>&1 | tail -10
✓ Doctor complete

🦞 OpenClaw installed successfully (2026.5.12)!
```
Note: error source shows `scripts/install.sh: line 3000` confirming guard did not fire.

Observed result after fix: piped execution completes successfully without truncated function names or hangs. Direct execution is unaffected. The pipe guard adds negligible overhead (one mktemp + cat + exec).
What was not tested: macOS bash 3.2 (BASH_SOURCE behavior may differ in very old bash versions). WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 (the reporter's environment) was not tested directly, but the fix is bash-level and platform-independent.

target.title: fix(agents): bound embedded compaction write locks
target.body:
Embedded auto-compaction can leave a live Gateway process holding a session write lock for the run-timeout window when the compaction promise does not settle, freezing the session without self-recovery.

Derived from a production incident; no matching upstream issue was found during the pre-push coverage search.

## Affected Surface

- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`
  - `runEmbeddedAttempt`
  - `resolveEmbeddedAttemptSessionWriteLockOptions`
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts`
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`

## Scope

This adjusts the embedded attempt session write-lock watchdog fallback so post-prompt compaction/session writes use the existing compaction timeout as their bounded fallback instead of inheriting the full run timeout.

This does not add a config field, does not change the global `session.writeLock.maxHoldMs` default, and does not remove locks externally. Explicit `session.writeLock.maxHoldMs` configuration and environment overrides still flow through `resolveSessionWriteLockOptions`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: embedded auto-compaction no longer makes the session write-lock watchdog inherit the full run-timeout window; the fallback is bounded by the existing compaction timeout.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux using the actual patched runtime module and local targeted test runner.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
node --import tsx -e "import { testing } from './src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts'; console.log(JSON.stringify(testing.resolveEmbeddedAttemptSessionWriteLockOptions({ config: {}, compactionTimeoutMs: 600000, env: {} })))"
pnpm test src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts
pnpm check:changed --base origin/main
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ node --import tsx -e "import { testing } from './src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts'; console.log(JSON.stringify(testing.resolveEmbeddedAttemptSessionWriteLockOptions({ config: {}, compactionTimeoutMs: 600000, env: {} })))"
{"timeoutMs":60000,"staleMs":1800000,"maxHoldMs":720000}
```

The copied live output comes from the patched OpenClaw runtime module. It shows the embedded attempt write-lock fallback resolves to `720000` ms for a `600000` ms compaction timeout rather than the default 48h run-timeout scale.

Observed result after fix: the embedded attempt session write-lock options now keep the standard acquire timeout and stale policy while bounding `maxHoldMs` to `compactionTimeoutMs + grace` through the existing `resolveSessionWriteLockOptions` / `resolveSessionLockMaxHoldFromTimeout` path.

What was not tested: a live Telegram or Weixin long-session reproduction was not re-run locally; the production incident evidence is included only as a redacted summary below.

### RED/GREEN regression

Before the fix, the new regression failed because the embedded attempt helper did not exist and the call site still derived `maxHoldMsFallback` from the run timeout plus compaction grace:

```text
$ pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts -t resolveEmbeddedAttemptSessionWriteLockOptions
...
FAIL  |agents| src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts > resolveEmbeddedAttemptSessionWriteLockOptions > bounds post-prompt session lock max hold to compaction timeout instead of run timeout
TypeError: testing.resolveEmbeddedAttemptSessionWriteLockOptions is not a function
...
Test Files  1 failed (1)
Tests  1 failed | 145 skipped (146)
```

After the fix:

```text
$ pnpm test src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  195 passed (195)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 35.25s
```

The new regression covers the 48h default run-timeout shape by asserting a 600000ms compaction timeout resolves to a 720000ms write-lock fallback, rather than a run-timeout-scale hold. The companion lifecycle test confirms a normally settling `session.compact()` still takes and releases the post-prompt write lock on the existing path.

### Production incident summary (redacted)

- T+0: embedded auto-compaction started for a long-lived direct session with a very large accumulated context.
- T+56m: no compaction complete event had appeared.
- The Gateway process was still alive and held an fd open for `<workspace>/agents/<agent>/sessions/<session-id>.jsonl.lock`.
- The lock payload shape was `{ "pid": "<redacted-pid>", "createdAt": "<redacted-timestamp>", "starttime": "<redacted-starttime>" }`.
- At the same time, `<session-id>.jsonl` was missing while `<session-id>.trajectory.jsonl` still existed, so the held lock was protecting a zombie transcript target.
- Restarting the Gateway released the lock, which restored the channel entry point.

Redacted fields: host/IP, real PID, real session id, full home path, Telegram identifiers, provider/model config, and raw transcript/trajectory content.

## Coverage Note

Pre-push open PR searches were run with independent queries and no `OR` syntax:

```text
gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "Fixes session write lock" --json number,title,url,body,updatedAt
[]

gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "session write lock embedded compaction" --json number,title,url,body,updatedAt
[]

gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "compaction promise unsettled" --json number,title,url,body,updatedAt
[]

gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "attempt session lock watchdog" --json number,title,url,body,updatedAt
[]

gh search prs --repo openclaw/openclaw --state open "pi-embedded-runner write lock" --json number,title,url,body,updatedAt
[]
```

No open PR was found covering the embedded compaction write-lock watchdog path.

## Verification

```text
$ pnpm test src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts

Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests  195 passed (195)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 35.25s
```

```text
$ pnpm check:changed --base origin/main
[check:changed] lanes=core, coreTests
[check:changed] conflict markers
[check:changed] changelog attributions
[check:changed] guarded extension wildcard re-exports
[check:changed] plugin-sdk wildcard re-exports
[check:changed] duplicate scan target coverage
[check:changed] dependency pin guard
[check:changed] package patch guard
[check:changed] typecheck core
[check:changed] typecheck core tests
[check:changed] lint core
[check:changed] media download helper guard
[check:changed] runtime sidecar loader guard
[check:changed] runtime import cycles
[check:changed] webhook body guard
[check:changed] pairing store guard
[check:changed] pairing account guard
```

```text
$ git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
<no output>

$ node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
<no output>

$ gitleaks protect --staged --redact
no leaks found
```

## What Was Not Tested

- A live Telegram or Weixin long-session reproduction was not re-run locally.
- The production host evidence was used only in redacted summary form; raw configs, transcript, trajectory, host identifiers, and channel identifiers were not copied into this PR.

target.title: Fix OpenAI compaction runtime routing
target.body:
## Summary
- Route direct embedded compaction through the selected OpenAI runtime provider before resolving model auth.
- Keep Codex-sized context budgeting for OpenAI model refs while using the Codex OAuth provider for the compaction LLM when the selected runtime is Codex.
- Add regression coverage for the OpenAI/Codex compaction auth route and for avoiding repeated preflight compaction after `tokensAfter` is persisted.

## Root Cause
OpenAI model refs such as `openai/gpt-5.5` can be selected with the Codex runtime, but `compactEmbeddedPiSessionDirectOnce` resolved the compaction model/auth from the raw OpenAI provider before applying the selected runtime provider contract. When budget preflight compaction fell back from stale/missing native Codex thread binding to context-engine compaction, it attempted direct OpenAI API-key auth and surfaced the missing `OPENAI_API_KEY` warning even though Codex OAuth was valid.

RCA proof:
- Before behavior: gateway logs showed repeated budget compaction for an OpenAI GPT-5.5 session ending with `No_API_key_found_for_provider_openai` after native harness compaction fell back to context-engine compaction.
- Code evidence: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts` used `resolvedCompactionTarget.provider` directly for `resolveModelAsync(...)` and `getApiKeyForModel(...)`, while normal run routing already had selected runtime provider handling.
- Regression chain: OpenAI refs were routed through Codex by default, compaction began using Codex runtime budgets, then required preflight compaction failures were surfaced instead of skipped.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: OpenAI GPT-5.5 compaction under the Codex runtime no longer asks for direct OpenAI API-key auth, and successful preflight compaction updates token state so the next reply does not repeat the same compaction.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout and managed gateway on Linux, Node 25.9.0, configured default model `openai/gpt-5.5` with Codex runtime/OAuth.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`; `pnpm build`; `pnpm openclaw gateway restart`; `pnpm openclaw gateway status`.

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest reported `Test Files 3 passed (3)` and `Tests 117 passed (117)`; build completed successfully; gateway restart completed and status reported `Runtime: running` with `Connectivity probe: ok`.

Observed result after fix: the compaction unit path resolves OpenAI/Codex runtime compaction through `openai-codex` before auth, while preserving OpenAI model config as the context-budget source.

What was not tested: no live Telegram reply was sent through the oversized production topic after the patch; the proof is targeted unit coverage plus local build and gateway restart/status.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm openclaw gateway restart`
- `pnpm openclaw gateway status`

target.title: fix(update): escape systemd update handoffs
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #84068 by launching systemd-managed update handoff helpers through `systemd-run --user --scope --collect` so the helper moves out of `openclaw-gateway.service` before the gateway unit restarts.
- Keeps launchd, schtasks, and non-systemd handoffs on the existing detached Node launch path.
- Adds a systemd-only 2s restart grace even when a caller asks for `restartDelayMs: 0`, plus focused coverage for the transient scope command and restart scheduling.

## Verification

- `pnpm install`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/update.test.ts src/commands/status-update-restart.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.ts src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/update.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/update.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --uncommitted`

Note: `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json ...` is blocked in this worktree before linting changed files by an unrelated WhatsApp boundary artifact failure: missing `baileys` types in `extensions/whatsapp`. Direct focused oxlint on changed files passed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: systemd-supervised Control UI/package update handoffs no longer leave the update helper inside `openclaw-gateway.service`, where the helper can be killed by the gateway unit restart before it writes `handoff.log` or runs `openclaw update`.

Real environment tested: WSL checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-84068-systemd-handoff`, Linux userspace, Node v24.15.0. The focused tests create a fake executable `systemd-run` and verify the production handoff path chooses `systemd-run --user --scope --collect --unit=openclaw-update-<handoff>.scope <node> <handoff.cjs> <handoff.json>` for `supervisor: "systemd"`, while preserving service identity env and stripping transient systemd invocation hints.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/update-managed-service-handoff.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/update.test.ts src/commands/status-update-restart.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix: The focused suite passed: 5 test files, 54 tests. New assertions cover transient systemd scope launch, handoff parameter generation, `OPENCLAW_SYSTEMD_UNIT` preservation, `INVOCATION_ID` stripping, and the minimum 2000ms systemd restart grace when `restartDelayMs: 0` is requested.

Observed result after fix: The systemd handoff starter spawns `systemd-run` instead of a plain detached Node child, and `update.run` schedules the gateway restart with enough grace for the transient scope handoff to leave the service cgroup.

What was not tested: I did not run a live package upgrade under a real `systemctl --user openclaw-gateway.service` unit in this turn. That should be the follow-up E2E before landing if required by maintainers.

target.title: fix(codex): keep idle guard after turn started
target.body:
## Summary

- keep the Codex app-server completion-idle watchdog armed after a current-turn `turn/started` notification
- add regression coverage for the reported `turn/started`-then-silence wedge
- update the existing pre-`turn/start` progress test to use real item progress instead of bare `turn/started`

Fixes #85251

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A Codex app-server turn that emits only current-turn `notification:turn/started` and then goes silent now keeps the short completion-idle watchdog armed and interrupts the turn instead of waiting for the outer stuck-session recovery window.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux in `/home/chenglunhu/code/openclaw`, exact PR head `0b7dd6ade620939ff4037ada98591d3344517ac9`, Node/Vitest project runtime from this repository.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the Codex app-server runtime harness against the exact PR head with `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`, then confirmed the new `turn/started`-only case returns the timeout path and calls `turn/interrupt`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the local run:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  205 passed (205)
   Start at  22:50:21
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The added regression exercises `runCodexAppServerAttempt` with a real app-server harness turn where the only current-turn notification is `turn/started`; the observed result is `timedOut=true`, prompt error `codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed`, and a `turn/interrupt` request for `thread-1` / `turn-1`.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live macOS Codex OAuth app-server process or a Telegram channel replay; this patch validates the source-proven app-server watchdog path directly.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`

- Audit C (broader rival): #75213 is an old conflicting bot PR in the same harness area but targets unrelated shared-client traffic; #85242 has rival #85296 and was skipped. No direct active PR fixes #85251.

target.title: Policy: add agent workspace conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a policy-only conformance surface for locking agent workspace posture down to read-only/no-write configurations.

- records agent/default sandbox `workspaceAccess` and tool deny coverage as policy evidence
- adds `agents.workspace.allowedAccess` and `agents.workspace.denyTools` checks
- documents the supported read-only policy shape, evidence payload, and attestation-hash impact

This is stacked after #81981 and intentionally stays at config/policy conformance level; it does not add runtime enforcement.

## Policy syntax

```jsonc
{
  "agents": {
    "workspace": {
      "allowedAccess": ["none", "ro"],
      "denyTools": ["exec", "process", "write", "edit", "apply_patch"],
    },
  },
}
```

Enabling or upgrading these rules adds `agentWorkspace` evidence to the workspace and attestation hashes. Operators should review the new evidence and refresh accepted attestation hashes after enabling these rules.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts --run --reporter=dot` (113 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts --run --reporter=dot` (12 passed)
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-policy-agent-workspace-rebased.tsbuildinfo`
- `env PATH="/tmp/npm-11-6-2-bin:$PATH" node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs --check` (matches CI npm 11.6.2)
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
- `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Policy conformance can require agent sandbox workspace access to stay within an allowlist and require explicit deny coverage for shell/process/file-mutation tools.
Real environment tested: WSL checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-policy-agent-workspace` on Ubuntu-24.04.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/policy-proof-85096/openclaw.json timeout 180 node --import tsx -e "import('./src/cli/run-main.ts').then(m=>m.runCli(['node','openclaw','policy','check','--json','--severity-min','error']))"`
Evidence after fix: Malformed policy shape produced `policy/policy-jsonc-invalid`; nonconforming config produced `policy/agents-workspace-access-denied` and `policy/agents-tool-not-denied`; accepted read-only posture produced `ok: true` with zero findings.
Observed result after fix: The policy check emitted `agentWorkspace` evidence and 31 policy checks, including the new agent workspace checks.
What was not tested: No runtime sandbox/tool enforcement was tested because this PR only adds config-level policy conformance.

### Redacted CLI evidence

Malformed policy:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/policy-jsonc-invalid",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "policy.jsonc agents.workspace.allowedAccess must be an array.",
      "target": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/allowedAccess"
    }
  ]
}
```

Nonconforming config:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/agents-workspace-access-denied",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "agents.defaults sandbox workspaceAccess 'rw' is not allowed by policy.",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/agents/defaults/sandbox/workspaceAccess",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/allowedAccess"
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/agents-tool-not-denied",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "agents.defaults does not deny required tool 'apply_patch'.",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/tools/deny",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/denyTools"
    }
  ]
}
```

Accepted read-only posture:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [],
  "agentWorkspace": [
    {
      "id": "agents-defaults-workspace-access",
      "kind": "workspaceAccess",
      "source": "oc://openclaw.config/agents/defaults/sandbox/workspaceAccess",
      "scope": "defaults",
      "value": "ro",
      "explicit": true
    },
    {
      "id": "agents-defaults-tool-apply_patch",
      "kind": "toolDeny",
      "source": "oc://openclaw.config/tools/deny",
      "scope": "defaults",
      "tool": "apply_patch",
```

target.title: fix(plugins): prevent openclaw update from silently downgrading managed deps
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85184. Prevents `openclaw update` from silently downgrading a newer already-installed managed direct dependency, and verifies the preserved version through the full install path so the intentional skip does not trigger a rollback.

## What changed

- Add a narrow downgrade guard in installPluginFromManagedNpmRoot: during `update`, if the incoming version is older than the version already recorded in the managed npm root, keep the installed version instead of overwriting it.
- Verify the post-install result against the preserved version (not the older incoming resolution) when the guard fires, so the kept dependency is not misread as a failed install and rolled back.
- Scope is intentionally narrow: only the unambiguous downgrade case during `update`. Non-downgrade pin rewrites and the broader respect-all-pins policy are left to maintainer decision.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw update` re-synced externalized bundled-plugin direct dependencies through installPluginFromManagedNpmRoot, which overwrote the managed npm root dependency and could silently downgrade a newer pinned/installed version. An earlier guard alone was insufficient: the post-install verification still compared the on-disk version against the older incoming resolution, so a preserved newer dependency was rolled back. This change guards the downgrade and aligns the verification with the preserved version.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js 24.14.0, current PR head. Exercised the full installPluginFromNpmSpec update path with vitest, plus focused unit tests for the predicate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the plugin install/update test suite under the real runtime via vitest.

Evidence after fix: stdout from the test run against the real runtime:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/install.test.ts
  install.test.ts (120 tests)
  shouldPreserveManagedDependencyAgainstDowngrade
    + preserves a newer installed dependency when update would downgrade it
    + catches patch-level downgrades / allows upgrade / allows same version / etc.
  installPluginFromNpmSpec downgrade guard (integration)
    + preserves a newer installed managed dependency without rollback during update
    + allows a normal upgrade to rewrite the managed dependency (no false preserve)
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  120 passed (120)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/update.test.ts src/infra/npm-managed-root.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts
  Test Files  3 passed (3)
  Tests  211 passed (211)
```

Observed result after fix: The integration test drives the full installPluginFromNpmSpec update path. With an installed 0.11.2 and an update resolving 0.10.0, the run completes successfully (no rollback) and the managed package.json keeps 0.11.2. A normal upgrade (installed 0.11.0, update 0.11.2) still rewrites to the newer version. All managed-root and update-cli acceptance suites pass unchanged.

What was not tested: A live mutating `openclaw update --tag` against a real registry was not run; the install/update path is exercised end to end with vitest using the real runtime and a mocked npm transport, matching the source-level reproduction in the issue.

target.title: Preserve Codex and Telegram context evidence
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram prompt context cached inbound messages but not OpenClaw's own outbound Telegram replies, so a later context window could include Keshav's 01:37 request while omitting the adjacent 01:39 assistant answer.
- Problem: normal streamed Telegram assistant finals are finalized through the draft-stream/edit path, so caching only `sendMessageTelegram` sends still missed the central streamed-final path.
- Problem: Codex per-turn context-engine projections were flattening prior tool calls/results to placeholders, hiding evidence needed to answer follow-up debugging questions.
- Solution: cache both successful Telegram outbound sends and preview-finalized streamed finals into the Telegram message-context cache, and preserve sanitized tool evidence in active context-engine projections.
- Scope boundary: this does not make every old transcript globally searchable; it fixes the observed Telegram outbound-context gap and selected-context payload elision only.

## Motivation

Keshav pointed out that a later Codex turn did not show the earlier timeout-halving work or the 01:39 final answer evidence. Rechecking the actual 10:11 session showed no user `/new` or `/reset`: the Telegram selected context included the 01:37 user request but omitted OpenClaw's own 01:39 Telegram reply. A separate later prompt also showed `tool call: bash [input omitted]` and `tool result: ... [content omitted]`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #85219
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram outbound replies now survive into later Telegram prompt-context windows; Codex context-engine per-turn projections no longer reduce prior tool work to empty placeholders.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this host, branch `fix/codex-context-tool-payloads`, head commit `8e3ea2be9e3462d3d60da14cf2290ccc796e2dc7`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran targeted Vitest coverage from the repo root. Also checked the production session logs that motivated the fix to confirm the before-state: 10:11 selected Telegram context contained Keshav's 01:37 request but omitted Kelaw's 01:39 reply; 10:18 Codex context contained only `[input omitted]` / `[content omitted]` tool placeholders.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Candidate runtime proof attached in [comment 4519833550](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85231#issuecomment-4519833550). The proof harness was temporary and removed after the run, leaving the PR diff unchanged.

  Candidate proof command:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/manual/pr-85231-candidate-proof.test.ts --reporter verbose
```

  Candidate proof result:

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       1 passed (1)
```

  Existing targeted regression run from the patched checkout:

```text
Test Files 4 passed (4)
Tests 218 passed (218)
```

- Observed result after fix: The candidate runtime proof invokes the production Telegram outbound-cache helper, reloads the persisted Telegram message cache, and shows the later selected prompt context includes the assistant outbound reply body. It also invokes the Codex context projection with `toolPayloadMode: "preserve"` and shows useful result text like `timeoutSeconds=7200` is retained while the raw shell command and fake API key are absent. The targeted regressions additionally cover normal sends, streamed-final preview finalization, and the run-attempt projection callsite.
- What was not tested: A full deployed Telegram-to-Codex production turn was not exercised. The current Mantis live Telegram catalog does not appear to include a scenario for internal prompt-context retention, so running a generic status/command scenario would not prove this PR's behavior. The attached candidate proof is the smallest available behavior proof without deploying this branch.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The captured 10:11 local Codex session prompt selected Keshav's 01:37 “halve those” message but not OpenClaw's 01:39 final answer. The captured 10:18 local Codex session prompt showed `tool call: bash [input omitted]` and `tool result: call_... [content omitted]`.

- Production Telegram media proof: GitHub-hosted screenshot of the reported 01:37 -> 01:39 -> 10:11 Telegram thread sequence.

  ![Telegram transcript showing the timeout-halving request, Kelaw confirmation, and later follow-up](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/keshavbotagent/openclaw/proof-pr-85231-telegram-screenshot/proof/pr-85231/telegram-context-proof.jpg)

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Telegram only recorded inbound messages in `message-cache.ts`; successful outbound sends were recorded for bot ownership/reactions but not for future prompt-context selection.
- Root cause: the first Telegram fix covered `sendMessageTelegram` sends but not preview-finalized streamed finals, which are delivered through draft-stream plus edit-message finalization.
- Root cause: `run-attempt.ts` passed `toolPayloadMode: contextEngineProjection ? "preserve" : "elide"`, so active per-turn context-engine projections used elision even when the projection engine had safe redaction support.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No regression covered Telegram outbound replies being present in later selected conversation context, streamed finals being written to the same cache, or active per-turn context-engine projections preserving redacted tool-result evidence.

## Prompt Boundary Review

ClawSweeper flagged the prompt-boundary expansion in `run-attempt.ts`. I reviewed the projection path and kept the current implementation because the fix needs prior tool-result evidence, not only tool metadata.

Boundary details:

- Tool-call inputs are not preserved as values; only the input shape and primitive types are rendered, so shell commands such as `cat .env` are omitted.
- Tool-result values are passed through `redactToolPayloadText` plus `redactSensitiveFieldValue`, which forces tool-mode redaction and applies built-in/user `logging.redactPatterns`.
- Rendered context is still selected by the context engine and capped by the projection budget; this PR does not add global transcript search.
- The remaining risk is intentional: non-secret private text in selected tool output may become model context. Narrowing that away would also remove the concrete evidence needed to avoid repeated work.

If maintainers do not want that boundary, the smallest alternative is to split this PR and merge only the Telegram outbound-cache fix while leaving Codex tool-result evidence elided.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: successful Telegram sends and preview-finalized streamed finals are recorded into the persisted prompt-context cache; active context-engine projection preserves useful tool-result evidence while redacting raw command/secret values.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers both Telegram delivery paths that can produce visible assistant replies and the Codex prompt sent to `turn/start`.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `context-engine-projection.test.ts` covers projection behavior directly; this PR adds callsite and Telegram outbound-cache coverage.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Future Telegram turns should be able to see OpenClaw's own recent Telegram replies in selected chat context, including streamed assistant finals, so a follow-up should not see only the user's earlier request without the bot answer. Codex should also have enough redacted prior tool-result context to avoid repeating completed work when that evidence exists in selected context.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/manual/pr-85231-candidate-proof.test.ts --reporter verbose` (temporary candidate proof harness, removed after run)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

## Summary

- Problem: normal streamed Telegram assistant finals are finalized through the draft-stream/edit path, so caching only `sendMessageTelegram` sends still missed the central streamed-final path.
- Problem: Codex per-turn context-engine projections were flattening prior tool calls/results to placeholders, hiding evidence needed to answer follow-up debugging questions.
- Solution: cache both successful Telegram outbound sends and preview-finalized streamed finals into the Telegram message-context cache, and preserve sanitized tool evidence in active context-engine projections.
- Scope boundary: this does not make every old transcript globally searchable; it fixes the observed Telegram outbound-context gap and selected-context payload elision only.

## Motivation

A separate later prompt also showed `tool call: bash [input omitted]` and `tool result: ... [content omitted]`.

target.title: fix(media): resolve media:// claim-check refs in loadImageFromRef and image-tool (#74123) [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `media://inbound/...` claim-check image refs were detected but then treated like workspace-relative paths in two native image-loading seams.
- Why it matters: agents could not recover an inbound image once a prompt or tool call carried the claim-check URI instead of a local file path.
- What changed: `loadImageFromRef` and the `image` tool now resolve inbound `media://` refs to their local media buffer before workspace path resolution.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no remote URL policy changes; no changelog edit.

AI-assisted: yes. Prompt/session reference: Pi goal session `2026-05-22T13-29-51Z`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #74123
- Related #74186
- Related #74278
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: when inbound images reached the native runner as `media://inbound/...` claim-check refs, image-loading seams resolved them as paths like `<workspace>/media:/inbound/...` and failed to load them.
- Real environment tested: self-hosted OpenClaw gateway using a direct channel image message.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: deployed the resolver wiring with the catalog fix, sent an image to the agent, then inspected the submitted provider trajectory and the visible channel reply.
- Evidence after fix (redacted copied runtime log):

```json
{
  "type": "prompt.submitted",
  "provider": "openai-codex",
  "modelId": "gpt-5.5",
  "data": {
    "imagesCount": 1,
    "msgContentTypes": ["image", "text"]
  }
}
```

Visible reply after fix:

```text
It’s a tan/cognac leather briefcase or laptop bag — structured rectangular shape, twin handles, detachable shoulder strap, zip top. The logo says “FOM” / “Freedom of Movement”.
```

- Observed result after fix: the agent could read and describe the inbound image.
- What was not tested: full channel E2E against every sandbox backend; focused sandbox bridge coverage now verifies native `media://` prompt-image loading.
- Before evidence (redacted copied runtime log):

```json
{
  "type": "prompt.submitted",
  "provider": "openai-codex",
  "modelId": "gpt-5.5",
  "data": {
    "prompt": "[media attached: media://inbound/<redacted>.jpg (image/jpeg)] ...",
    "imagesCount": 0
  }
}
```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `media://inbound/...` refs were recognized as image refs, but these two consumers did not resolve the claim-check URI before generic workspace path resolution.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered detection and other seams, but not native `loadImageFromRef` or `image` tool resolution of `media://` refs.
- Contributing context (if known): this complements #74186 and #74278 by wiring two additional seams from #74123.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/images.test.ts`, `src/agents/tools/image-tool.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `media://inbound/...` refs are resolved through the media store before workspace path fallback, including native prompt-image loading with sandbox reads enabled.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: both failures were seam-local URI resolution gaps.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none for these two seams.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Agents can recover inbound media claim-check image refs in native prompt image loading and the `image` tool instead of failing on a mangled workspace path.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
media://inbound/id.jpg -> workspace path fallback -> <workspace>/media:/inbound/id.jpg -> ENOENT

After:
media://inbound/id.jpg -> media store resolver -> local media buffer path -> image loads
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux container deployment
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw gateway
- Model/provider: `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`
- Integration/channel (if any): direct channel image message
- Relevant config (redacted): standard inbound media storage with claim-check `media://inbound/...` refs

### Steps

1. Produce an inbound image ref as `media://inbound/<id>.jpg`.
2. Let the native image loader or `image` tool consume that ref.
3. Confirm the ref resolves through the media store instead of workspace path fallback.

### Expected

- The image loader receives a local media buffer path and loads the image.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the URI was resolved as a workspace-relative path containing `media:/inbound/...` and failed to load.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: live direct-channel image turn reached the model as `imagesCount: 1`; the model replied with a correct visual description.
- Edge cases checked: native prompt image loading, native prompt image loading with sandbox reads enabled, and `image` tool path resolution each have focused regression coverage.
- What you did **not** verify: full channel E2E against every sandbox backend.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: malformed or missing `media://` refs could be mistaken for file paths.
  - Mitigation: unresolved media refs stay on the existing failure path rather than being silently treated as valid files.

target.title: [Bug] Fix Telegram message-tool reply previews causing disappearing messages
target.body:
## Summary

Refs #80520.

This fixes one Telegram visible-reply path where ordinary turns configured for message-tool-only replies could still create Telegram draft/preview callbacks before the shared channel-message source-reply policy suppressed final source delivery.

- Resolve the source reply delivery mode with `resolveChannelMessageSourceReplyDeliveryMode` before enabling Telegram draft streaming.
- When that shared policy resolves to `message_tool_only`, skip Telegram partial/reasoning preview callbacks and pass `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` into the reply dispatcher.
- Preserve automatic source delivery for authorized explicit commands, and keep unauthorized group slash text on the message-tool-only path.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram agent acknowledgement / preview messages could appear briefly and then disappear when visible replies were configured for `message_tool`. This matches the reporter repro in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80520#issuecomment-4510305367: `Send me 3 test messages` produced a disappearing acknowledgement while the three durable Telegram messages remained.

- **Real environment tested**: A real local OpenClaw install using the Telegram extension against an actual Telegram direct message chat with `@JIMCLAW_bot`, on May 22, 2026 in America/Chicago. The live setup had `messages.visibleReplies = "message_tool"` and `messages.groupChat.visibleReplies = "message_tool"`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Applied the patch to the local OpenClaw install, restarted the local OpenClaw gateway, then sent this real Telegram message to the bot: `Send 3 more test messages and and acknowledgement`. After that, checked the local OpenClaw runtime log around the 08:58 test window for inbound/outbound Telegram delivery entries and draft/delete cleanup entries.

- **Evidence after fix**: Reporter-provided after-fix Telegram screenshots show three durable bot replies at 8:58 AM with no disappearing acknowledgement bubble: `/home/jim/Downloads/Screenshot_20260522_085940_Telegram.jpg` and `/home/jim/Downloads/Screenshot_20260522_085944_Telegram.jpg`. Redacted runtime log excerpt from the patched live OpenClaw setup:

  ```text
  08:58:40.806 inbound telegram:<redacted-user-id> -> @JIMCLAW_bot direct message
  08:58:49.557 telegram outbound send ok messageId=814
  08:58:49.563 gateway/ws message.action success channel=telegram
  08:58:49.914 telegram outbound send ok messageId=815
  08:58:49.920 gateway/ws message.action success channel=telegram
  08:58:50.833 telegram outbound send ok messageId=816
  08:58:50.839 gateway/ws message.action success channel=telegram
  ```

  A log search around that proof window found no Telegram draft/delete cleanup entries for the successful patched test.

- **Observed result after fix**: Telegram displayed exactly three durable bot replies: `Test message 1 of 3 ✅`, `Test message 2 of 3 — sequential delivery confirmed`, and `Test message 3 of 3 — all three sent with acknowledgement`. The disappearing acknowledgement/preview behavior did not recur, and the reporter confirmed: “it looks like its working. No disappearing messages.”

- **What was not tested**: Full repository-wide build/check/test was not run locally because `pnpm` is not available on PATH on this machine. I did run the focused Telegram dispatcher Vitest file directly, plus the real Telegram live setup proof above.

## Related Issue Comments

- User repro comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80520#issuecomment-4510305367
- Related canonical no-send/drop evidence:
  - https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80520#issuecomment-4455610365
  - https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80520#issuecomment-4470879235
  - https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80520#issuecomment-4492527435
  - https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80520#issuecomment-4492534915

## Validation

- `git diff --check` passed.

target.title: Policy: add agent workspace conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a policy-only conformance surface for locking agent workspace posture down to read-only/no-write configurations.

- records agent/default sandbox `workspaceAccess` and tool deny coverage as policy evidence
- adds `agents.workspace.allowedAccess` and `agents.workspace.denyTools` checks
- documents the supported read-only policy shape, evidence payload, and attestation-hash impact

This is stacked after #81981 and intentionally stays at config/policy conformance level; it does not add runtime enforcement.

## Policy syntax

```jsonc
{
  "agents": {
    "workspace": {
      "allowedAccess": ["none", "ro"],
      "denyTools": ["exec", "process", "write", "edit", "apply_patch"],
    },
  },
}
```

Enabling or upgrading these rules adds `agentWorkspace` evidence to the workspace and attestation hashes. Operators should review the new evidence and refresh accepted attestation hashes after enabling these rules.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts --run --reporter=dot` (113 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts --run --reporter=dot` (12 passed)
- `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test-policy-agent-workspace-rebased.tsbuildinfo`
- `env PATH="/tmp/npm-11-6-2-bin:$PATH" node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs --check` (matches CI npm 11.6.2)
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
- `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Policy conformance can require agent sandbox workspace access to stay within an allowlist and require explicit deny coverage for shell/process/file-mutation tools.
Real environment tested: WSL checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-policy-agent-workspace` on Ubuntu-24.04.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/policy-proof-85096/openclaw.json timeout 180 node --import tsx -e "import('./src/cli/run-main.ts').then(m=>m.runCli(['node','openclaw','policy','check','--json','--severity-min','error']))"`
Evidence after fix: Malformed policy shape produced `policy/policy-jsonc-invalid`; nonconforming config produced `policy/agents-workspace-access-denied` and `policy/agents-tool-not-denied`; accepted read-only posture produced `ok: true` with zero findings.
Observed result after fix: The policy check emitted `agentWorkspace` evidence and 31 policy checks, including the new agent workspace checks.
What was not tested: No runtime sandbox/tool enforcement was tested because this PR only adds config-level policy conformance.

### Redacted CLI evidence

Malformed policy:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/policy-jsonc-invalid",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "policy.jsonc agents.workspace.allowedAccess must be an array.",
      "target": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/allowedAccess"
    }
  ]
}
```

Nonconforming config:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [
    {
      "checkId": "policy/agents-workspace-access-denied",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "agents.defaults sandbox workspaceAccess 'rw' is not allowed by policy.",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/agents/defaults/sandbox/workspaceAccess",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/allowedAccess"
    },
    {
      "checkId": "policy/agents-tool-not-denied",
      "severity": "error",
      "message": "agents.defaults does not deny required tool 'apply_patch'.",
      "ocPath": "oc://openclaw.config/tools/deny",
      "requirement": "oc://policy.jsonc/agents/workspace/denyTools"
    }
  ]
}
```

Accepted read-only posture:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "checksRun": 31,
  "findings": [],
  "agentWorkspace": [
    {
      "id": "agents-defaults-workspace-access",
      "kind": "workspaceAccess",
      "source": "oc://openclaw.config/agents/defaults/sandbox/workspaceAccess",
      "scope": "defaults",
      "value": "ro",
      "explicit": true
    },
    {
      "id": "agents-defaults-tool-apply_patch",
      "kind": "toolDeny",
      "source": "oc://openclaw.config/tools/deny",
      "scope": "defaults",
      "tool": "apply_patch",
      "denied": true,
      "explicit": true
    }
  ]
}
```

target.title: Fix Codex responses empty output failover
target.body:
## Summary
- Detect Codex Responses attempts that spend output tokens but produce no visible assistant output.
- Route that case through the existing failover path as `empty_response`.
- Add regression coverage for issue #85364.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Codex Responses attempts that report nonzero output tokens but do not surface any visible assistant message now enter the failover path as `empty_response` instead of returning the generic "Agent couldn't generate a response" payload.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch `codex/codex-responses-empty-output-failover`, with the pushed PR head `73e5beafae`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the focused pi-embedded runner command after applying the patch:
  `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.codex-server-error-fallback.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout after the fix:

  ```text
  RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/zuozheng/Documents/Codex/2026-05-22/openclaw-openclaw-contribute-3/openclaw

  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  2 passed (2)
    Start at  22:37:47
    Duration  3.87s (transform 1.82s, setup 134ms, import 269ms, tests 3.28s, environment 0ms)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: The new regression case rejects with `FailoverError` and the synthetic error message `openai-codex-responses returned no visible assistant output despite nonzero output tokens.`, proving the empty visible-output condition now reaches the configured fallback path.
